question: Describe the configuration of the NuScale control room.

nuscale_ch01_0852.title: SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0: Crediting Manual Operator Actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) AnalysesC.1.B Review Criteria for Phase 1 (Analysis)
nuscale_ch01_0852.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for crediting manual operator actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) analyses, as outlined in SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0. It confirms that NuScale’s approach aligns with the updated review criteria for all phases of D3 analysis, superseding previous guidance, while noting that certain aspects of probabilistic risk assessment remain the responsibility of the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0853.title: SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0: Crediting Manual Operator Actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) AnalysesC.2.B Review Criteria for Phase 2 (Preliminary Validation)
nuscale_ch01_0853.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding the crediting of manual operator actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) analyses, as outlined in SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0. It confirms that NuScale meets the review criteria for all phases (analysis, preliminary validation, integrated system validation, and long-term integrity), superseding previous guidance, while noting that certain aspects of probabilistic risk assessment remain the responsibility of the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0110.title: Figure 1.7-1b: Electrical Symbols
nuscale_ch01_0110.summary: This section provides a comprehensive legend of electrical symbols and abbreviations used in NuScale’s Final Safety Analysis Report drawings, including representations for switches, relays, indicators, and logic gates. It explains the meaning of various symbols, color codes, and logic functions, ensuring clarity and consistency in interpreting electrical and instrumentation diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0038.title: Main Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0038.summary: The Main Control Room (MCR) is located below grade in the Control Room Building (CRB) and includes various support spaces such as offices, conference rooms, and equipment rooms. It houses control panels for all installed NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), allowing reactor operators to monitor and control multiple NPMs using digital control systems that ensure independence between safety and nonsafety systems. The MCR provides comprehensive monitoring, alarms, and controls for plant operations, with additional display stations for shared systems and diagnostics.

nuscale_ch01_0370.title: SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3: Control Room Area Ventilation SystemII.2 Environmental and Dynamic Effects
nuscale_ch01_0370.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3 requirements for the Control Room Area Ventilation System, specifically regarding environmental and dynamic effects. It states that NuScale meets the regulatory criteria, with no exceptions noted for this aspect.

nuscale_ch01_0372.title: SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3: Control Room Area Ventilation SystemII.4 Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0372.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3 requirements for the Control Room Area Ventilation System, confirming compliance with criteria for protection against natural phenomena, environmental effects, and sharing of systems. It notes that the control room ventilation system does not use emergency filtration for accident conditions but instead supplies clean air from compressed air tanks to protect operators.

nuscale_ch01_0062.title: Control Rod Assembly Drop and Control Rod Drive Shaft Alignment Test
nuscale_ch01_0062.summary: This section describes testing conducted to validate the performance, reliability, and repeatability of the NuScale control rod assembly (CRA) drop and drive shaft alignment, including remote connect/disconnect operations. The tests, performed at Curtiss Wright facilities, demonstrated that the remote disconnect mechanism is reliable and repeatable, and the results have informed improvements in the design, though they do not establish the final design basis. The section also outlines the multiple support features used to align the longer control rod drive shafts in the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams, legends, and tables provided in the FSAR, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be supplied by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0037.title: Control Building
nuscale_ch01_0037.summary: The section describes the refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM), which are conducted independently for each module. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling area using specialized cranes and tools, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to service after successful testing and inspection.

nuscale_ch01_0224.title: DSRS 7.2.6, Rev 0: Derivation of System InputsAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0224.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with various regulatory criteria and standards for digital instrumentation and control (I&C) systems, noting areas of full or partial compliance with specific Regulatory Guides (RGs) and IEEE standards. For DSRS 7.2.6, Rev 0, regarding the derivation of system inputs, the document states that all relevant criteria are met and there are no specific DSRS acceptance criteria for this item.

nuscale_ch01_0225.title: DSRS 7.2.7, Rev 0: Setpoints II.1 Conformance to RG 1.105
nuscale_ch01_0225.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides and standards for digital instrumentation and control (I&C) systems. It details which criteria are fully or partially met for equipment qualification, reliability, protective actions, interlocks, and system input derivation, referencing specific regulatory guides and IEEE standards. Partial conformance is noted for some criteria, with further details provided in referenced tables.

nuscale_ch01_0164.title: SRP 6.4, Rev 3: Control Room Habitability SystemII.7 Toxic Gas Hazards
nuscale_ch01_0164.summary: The NuScale control room habitability system does not rely on traditional emergency ventilation but instead uses compressed air tanks to ensure clean air during accidents, eliminating the risk of toxic gas or radioactive material ingress via ventilation. The guidance on toxic gas hazards is only partially applicable, as programmatic requirements are the responsibility of the COL applicant. Additionally, NuScale does not use engineered safety feature (ESF) filter or ventilation systems for accident mitigation, and such systems are not credited in their dose analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0161.title: SRP 6.4, Rev 3: Control Room Habitability SystemII.5 Relative Location of Source and Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0161.summary: The NuScale design does not rely on traditional control room emergency ventilation systems for habitability during accidents; instead, it uses compressed air tanks to provide clean air, eliminating the risk of contamination via ventilation inlets. As a result, several regulatory criteria related to ventilation-based habitability and ESF filtration systems are not applicable, though some dose guideline requirements partially apply and toxic gas hazard responsibilities are deferred to the COL applicant.

edges.description: Section A outlines the review criteria for Phase 1 (Analysis) of crediting manual operator actions in D3 analyses, while Section B provides the review criteria for Phase 2 (Preliminary Validation) of the same process, making Phase 1 a prerequisite for Phase 2 in the regulatory review sequence.
